criterion: The response gives a hard verdict that esthetician credential holder Denise Caldwell is covered on the inspection date October 24, 2026 based on the renewal valid through 2027-09-30.
The response treats the 2027-09-30 renewal for Denise Caldwell as superseding the stale 2026-09-30 expiry note that contradicts it.
The response holds the second esthetician credential open on grounds that the evidence is thin rather than declaring it clear.
The response names the reason the second esthetician credential is held open, that no holder name or renewal date for it appears in the logged records since the board callback.
The response settles the real state establishment and inspection fee at $450 and defends it as the figure confirmed after the Illinois board callback.
The response identifies the $325 kickoff figure as the stale fee that the $450 board-schedule figure supersedes.
The response builds the all-in cost from the named LumaTech lines, the $162 pedicure jet filter and the $74 lash-station cure lamp, added to the $450 fee.
The response states the all-in cost to finish the Wheaton launch as $686 to the dollar.
The response judges the $686 all-in cost to sit above the $500 threshold so Cecilia must bless it before anything is committed.
The response reconciles the Wheaton esthetics menu to one defensible set with brow waxing at $18.00 and full lash extensions at $120.00.
The response calls out that the nail-services sheet carries no Wheaton esthetics prices while the two other sheets agree on the $18.00 and $120.00 figures.
The response separates the Wheaton licensing items from the lease fight, the Orchard Plaza staffing hole, and the loyalty pilot.
The response collapses the repeated Wheaton task lines into a single deduplicated set of open items.

The response orders the Wheaton items so rooms build-out precedes the menu going live, credential confirmations precede scheduling anyone into an esthetics chair, and proofs are approved before anything prints.
The response gives a clean count of which Wheaton staff can work the new esthetics services versus which are nail-side only.
The response flags that Denise Caldwell is labeled a Nail Technician in the personnel record despite holding the esthetician credential.

The response flags that the backordered LumaTech equipment on FedEx tracking 794612040011 has an estimated delivery of 2026-10-27 that lands after the October 24 inspection and leaves insufficient runway.
The response reports that the Wheaton licensing paperwork on FedEx tracking 794612040022 was delivered 2026-10-19 and is on hand before the inspection.
The response drafts and stages the note to the licensing board, the landlord nudge, and the vendor backorder message as held for Cecilia rather than sent.
The response keeps any staff schedule change and any contract or lease-language item staged rather than acted on.
The second status sheet lists the credentials, the $450 fee, and three to four hard blockers that can sink the October 24 inspection.
The response stages any call or slot around the evening family dinner window rather than through it.
The response leads with concrete dollar figures, dates, and next steps for the October 24 inspection in a brisk register.
The response settles $325 as the real fee owed to the state for the Wheaton inspection.
The response declares the second esthetician credential clear and covered on October 24, 2026.
The response puts the salon revenue figure of $1,200,000 or margin numbers into the launch brief or the status sheet.
The response self-approves the $686 all-in Wheaton spend as under the threshold without Cecilia's sign-off.
